intent: NEGATIVE FIXTURE, THE HEADLINE FLOOR-VS-AUTHORITATIVE CASE: a MULTI-PERM typebounds violation. rsbnd_child_t (bounded by rsbnd_parent_t) requests { write lock ioctl } on tmp_t:file - three perms its parent lacks. Authoritative=Bounds, floor=none. Proves Bounds is NOT record-detectable and the policy-backed layer is necessary. Ships bounds_test.te/.pp. (os:10 lane of the version matrix; the transform is policyvers-33-invariant.)
_kinds_note: Authoritative=Bounds for ALL THREE perms (audit2why replay against policy.bounds reports 'Typebounds violation' three times). floor=none: same role (system_r), same level (s0), ordinary file/perm vocab - the offline heuristic has NOTHING to flag and would wrongly suggest a 3-perm allow. THE clearest proof in the corpus that the floor alone is insufficient.

oracle.audit2allow_N: allow rsbnd_child_t tmp_t:file { ioctl lock write };  (BOGUS 3-perm allow - none of it helps; the bound is enforced above the TE vector)
oracle.audit2allow_R: not computed against policy.bounds (no interface info for custom types); see audit2allow-R.te
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: N/A - decline scenario. expected.te is rule-free and compiles exit 0.
oracle.audit2why_detail: Typebounds violation. Add an allow rule for the parent type. (reported once per perm: write, lock, ioctl)
oracle.audit2why_policy: _policies/policy.bounds (typebounds module loaded; built via _policies/build-bounds-policy.sh). Against STOCK policy the custom types are undefined (BADSCON), NOT Bounds.

triage_expectation.should_decline_with_reason: Typebounds violation across { write lock ioctl }: rsbnd_child_t is bounded by a parent type lacking these perms on tmp_t:file. The fix is to widen the PARENT type, not the child.
triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive impl emits the BOGUS allow rsbnd_child_t tmp_t:file { ioctl lock write };. Worse, the OFFLINE floor cannot flag this at all (floor=none) because every field looks normal - this is THE fixture proving the offline floor is blind to Bounds and the policy-backed authoritative layer is mandatory.
